mission.title: kcp: ✨ Support for local cluster services DNS resolution
mission.description:
## Summary

Configure synced deployments DNS Config to point to kcp DNS resolver mapping local namespaces to physical namespaces for cluster local services.
 
## Related issue(s)

Fixes #505 
Fixes #1465

resolution.summary:
@lionelvillard, thanks for the PR! 

A couple of comments:

I see that the PR doesn't take into account the workspace of the Pod that is making the request, so that could lead to conflicts in the namespace resolution, given the following scenario:

1. Pod A running in Namespace A belongs to workspace A
1. Service B in Namespace B belongs to workspace B
1. Service B in Namespace B belongs to workspace A

if POD A tries to resolve the service B in namespace B. Currently, the plugin can respond with a service from another Workspace (B)

To solve this, the plugin has to be aware of the Workspace of the querying POD and filter accordingly.

Also, wiring up the Syncer PODs IP to the dnsConfig means that if the syncer pod gets recreated (update, scale...), all the currently deployed pods/deployments will need to be resynced. Perhaps a service that fronts the syncer deployment with a cluster IP could be a better solution?

Thank you!
steps: Pod A running in Namespace A belongs to workspace A
Service B in Namespace B belongs to workspace B
Service B in Namespace B belongs to workspace A
